The birds along the western ridge went silent about a week ago and have not come back
Something has been digging up the ground near the old stones at night
The water in the main stream shifted color for a day and then went back to normal
A bloom of wildflowers appeared along the path where nothing has grown in years
The fish in the reservoir have been jumping at odd hours when there are no insects about
An old rockslide shifted again and exposed what looks like a passage cut into the stone
The moss on the north wall has spread twice as far this season as anyone has seen before
Deer have been coming closer to the settlement than usual and do not startle the way they should
The spring that feeds the eastern cistern slowed to a trickle and no one knows what changed upstream
A formation of large birds passed overhead in a pattern nobody here recognized
The ground near the old foundation stays warm to the touch even on cold nights
Mushrooms appeared in a perfect line running from the gate to the old well overnight
I found tracks near the storehouse this morning. Big ones, and fresh
The frogs in the pond have been louder than usual at night. Hard to sleep near the east side
A tree that has been standing since before the settlement was founded split apart without any storm to explain it
The berry bushes along the path produced twice the crop this year and nobody planted new ones
I saw a snake in the root cellar yesterday. First one anyone has found inside the walls in years
The ants have been building mounds larger than I have ever seen. The old-timers say it means a harsh season is coming
Something stripped the bark off three trees in a straight line near the northern boundary
The creek bed has shifted course by about two paces. The water just found a new way around the stones
A hawk has been circling the settlement every morning at the same time for a week now
The soil near the old dump site smells different than it used to. Sharper, almost chemical
I noticed animal burrows appearing in the embankment near the wall. Could undermine the foundation if they dig deep enough
The weeds in the common garden have been growing faster than anyone can pull them
A dead tree fell across the path to the creek. It was sound enough last season, but the rot went through it fast
The tadpoles in the pond are the largest I have seen. Something in the water is feeding them well
There are claw marks on the fence posts near the livestock pen. Too high for anything we keep around here
The owls have moved closer to the settlement and their calls carry through the whole place at night
A patch of ground near the eastern gate has sunk about a hand's width. The soil must be washing away underneath
The wildflower bloom happened a full month early this year and it caught the bees off guard too
I found shed fur caught on the fence that does not match any animal we keep
The stream has a layer of foam on it that was not there last week. Something upstream must have changed
A vine has been climbing the storehouse wall and it has covered the window in less than a season
The bats that roost in the old tower have doubled in number. Good for the insects, unsettling for the watch
Something large has been moving through the underbrush at the edge of the cleared land. The tracks are unclear
The pine resin has been running heavier than usual this year. The trees look stressed
I saw a fox in the middle of the day near the market. They are usually more careful than that
The algae in the cistern came back within a week of being cleaned. Whatever feeds it is persistent
An entire colony of ground-nesting birds abandoned their site near the south field without obvious cause
The roots of the old tree in the square have lifted the paving stones another inch since last season
A spider has built a web across the workshop door three mornings in a row. It gets swept away and rebuilt each night
The moss on the stones near the well has turned from green to a deep brown. The healer thinks the water chemistry changed
Something has been pulling fish out of the holding pool. Not an animal we recognize from the marks left behind
The hedgerow along the south boundary has gaps now that were not there at the start of the season
I counted more rabbit warrens this spring than any year prior. The population is booming
A woodpecker has been hammering at the same support beam for days. The wood must be full of something
The lichen on the gateposts changed from grey to orange over the course of a week
There are drag marks in the mud near the creek that suggest something heavy moved through in the night
The birds started singing before dawn today by at least an hour. The whole settlement noticed
A cluster of toadstools appeared around the base of every fence post on the east side. Same species, all at once
The deer trails that used to run east of the settlement have shifted south. Something must have changed their route
I found a feather near the gate that is longer than my forearm. No one could identify the bird
The tree canopy has thickened so much this season that the path to the creek is in shadow all day now
Something burrowed under the tool shed and the floor has started to sag in one corner
The cattails along the reservoir have taken over a section that was open water last year
A family of ravens settled on the hall roof and they watch everyone who comes and goes
The ground squirrels have been hoarding more aggressively than usual. They know something about the season ahead
I noticed fresh scratches on the tree near the crossroads. High up, about twice my height
The fireflies have been absent this year. First time anyone can recall not seeing them at all
A fallen log near the path has been colonized by more varieties of fungus than the healer can identify
The water level in the well has been dropping steadily even though the rains have been normal
Something killed a patch of grass in a perfect circle near the old stones. The ground inside the circle is bare
The crows have been gathering in larger groups than usual. Hundreds of them in the trees at dusk
A turtle showed up in the drainage ditch and has apparently decided to stay. The children are delighted
The brambles along the fence line have thorns longer than any I have seen before
I heard something howling at dawn three days running. Too deep for a dog, wrong rhythm for a wolf
The wildflowers along the main road are growing in two distinct colors this year, split right down the middle of the path
A section of the creek bank collapsed and exposed a layer of clay that the potters are very interested in
The caterpillars have been so numerous this season that certain trees are nearly stripped bare
Something has been gnawing at the wooden supports under the bridge. The tooth marks are wide
The pollen has been thick enough to leave a yellow film on every surface outdoors
I found animal tracks overlapping in the mud near the gate. At least four different species, all using the same path
The old stump in the square sprouted new growth after being dead for years. Nobody can explain it
A stillness settled over the area yesterday. No birdsong, no insects, no wind. Lasted about an hour and then everything resumed
The earthworms have been coming to the surface in unusual numbers. The ground might be too wet below
Something peeled the bark off a standing tree in a spiral pattern. Not an animal behavior anyone here recognizes
The nest in the eaves of the storehouse has been there for generations. Same species returns every year without fail
A school of fish appeared in the shallow part of the creek where the water is usually too fast for them
The ivy on the old wall has started producing berries for the first time in anyone's memory
I noticed the morning cobwebs have been unusually elaborate lately. Intricate patterns spanning the entire gate frame
The moles have torn up the common field so badly it looks like someone took a plow to it
A pair of herons settled at the reservoir and have been standing motionless for hours at a stretch
The groundwater seepage near the north wall has left mineral deposits on the stones. White streaks running down the face
Something has been eating the bark off the young saplings we planted along the boundary. Every single one
The swallows arrived three weeks late this season and in smaller numbers than anyone expected
A stand of reeds appeared along the drainage channel that was clear stone last year
The snails have been climbing the walls of the storehouse every night and leaving trails across the doors
I watched a pair of eagles hunting over the open ground yesterday. First time they have come this close
The leaf litter is deeper this year than usual. The trees shed early and heavily
A patch of clover has taken over the corner of the common garden and the bees are all over it
The crickets went silent in the middle of the night and did not start again until well after dawn
Something left a pile of fish bones near the creek bank. Neat stack, like it was sorted
The hornets built a nest in the wall cavity near the gate. The watch has been giving it a wide berth
I found a burrow entrance near the foundation that is wider than my fist. Whatever lives there is not small
The tree line has crept noticeably closer to the cleared ground over the past few seasons
A bloom of algae turned the reservoir green overnight and cleared up just as fast two days later
The local songbirds have been imitating sounds they should not know. One of them does a passable gate hinge
The undergrowth near the path has thickened to the point where the old shortcut is impassable now
Something has been rolling stones into the creek. They are too heavy for the current and too regular to be natural
The night insects have been louder this season than anyone can remember. You can hear them from inside with the windows closed
A vine growing along the fence produced a flower none of the gardeners can identify. Not from around here
The animal trails through the brush are wider and more worn than they were last year. More traffic, or bigger animals
I saw tracks in the soft ground near the reservoir that had six toes. Not sure what leaves a print like that
The salamanders in the creek have moved to the shallow pools. Usually means the main channel is about to change
A chunk of the hillside slumped after the rain and exposed roots that go deeper than anyone thought the trees reached
The milkweed along the fence has been covered in monarch butterflies. More than I have seen in years
Something stripped every berry from the bushes along the north path in a single night. Clean, not a branch damaged
The mud near the gate has a sulfur smell that comes and goes. Strongest in the morning
I heard frogs calling from a spot where there is no standing water. Either there is a pool nobody knows about or they have moved
The spiders have been building webs between every post and rail. Walk anywhere at dawn and you are wearing silk by the time you get back
A tree near the wall dropped a limb without wind or rot. The break was clean, like it just let go
The lizards on the south wall have been basking in larger groups than usual. Dozens of them, barely moving
I found a patch of ground where nothing grows. No obvious reason. The soil looks the same as everywhere else
The dragonflies over the pond have been enormous this season. Wingspan as wide as my hand
Something has been stripping the lower branches of every tree along the boundary. Clean breaks, all at the same height
The worms in the compost heap are twice the size they were last year. The gardener says the soil is unusually rich
A pair of foxes denned under the old woodpile and nobody wants to disturb them because they keep the mice down
The algae bloom in the creek has a faintly luminous quality at night. Subtle, but several people have noticed it
I saw a column of ants crossing the main path in a line so straight it looked measured. Thousands of them, perfectly ordered
The hedgehogs have moved into the garden and the gardener is torn between the pest control and the damage to the beds
